steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim is that Tokarczuk's novel resembles classic magical realist novels in its juxtaposition of literary realism with Polish folklore. A supporting quotation must show both elements — realism and folklore — in direct contrast with each other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The logic of the realistic plot of Primeval and Other Times is repeatedly and productively disrupted by the presence of imagery and situations drawn from Polish folklore.': directly shows literary realism (the realistic plot) placed in tension with Polish folklore (disrupting imagery/situations) — exactly the juxtaposition the claim describes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Although Polish folklore clearly informs the style ... the novel also shows the inarguable influence of the magical realist tradition of Latin America.': shifts focus to Latin American influence rather than demonstrating the realism-vs.-folklore juxtaposition. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Like many works in the Latin American magical realist tradition, Primeval and Other Times is indebted to antirealistic elements in the folkloric tradition of Poland.': describes folkloric influence but frames it as debt to Latin American tradition, not a juxtaposition of realism and folklore. ✗
  • Choice D — 'While Primeval and Other Times alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes ... details suggesting Polish folklore nevertheless occur throughout.': notes alternation but does not clearly show realism and folklore in direct juxtaposition. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
